question_text: A sharp knife cuts through bread more easily than a blunt knife, even when the same force is applied. Explain why the sharp knife exerts more pressure.
correct_answer: Because pressure = force ÷ area, and the sharp knife has a smaller contact area, so the same force is concentrated over a smaller area, creating higher pressure.
explanation: Pressure is calculated by dividing force by the area it acts over. A sharp knife has a very small contact area with the bread, so when you apply force to it, that force is concentrated onto a tiny area, creating much higher pressure. A blunt knife spreads the same force over a larger area, resulting in lower pressure and making it harder to cut.
